# Dilly's Digicam

> Drop your photos, pick your favs, and Dilly will help give them that 00s digicam aesthetic.

A client side photo editor that applies a late 90s/2000s disposable camera aesthetic to your images. Everything runs in the browser; no uploads, no server.

## How It Works

1. Drop or select photos (supports JPEG, PNG, HEIC)
2. Pick the ones you want to edit
3. Photos are processed with a compact camera flash effect: green/yellow colour cast, flash falloff simulation, fine grain with colour noise, date stamp, and JPEG compression
4. Download individually or as a zip

## Technical Details

- Built with Alpine.js, Tailwind CSS, and Parcel
- HEIC files are converted to JPEG client side using heic-to
- Image processing uses the Canvas API (colour grading, flash falloff, fine grain, date stamp overlay)
- Output is resized to 1600px on the long edge and exported as JPEG at 72% quality
- Zip downloads powered by fflate

## Source

- Repository: https://github.com/revett/dillysdigicam
- License: MIT
